claims.claim: Conor McGregor has a history of fighting through injuries during fight week.
claims.quote: he's fought through injuries and stuff. It's just like he's here to collect his check. He's never pulled out on a fight week type of situation.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has not won a professional fight in over six and a half years.
claims.quote: he's not won in seven years, six and a half.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has not competed in five years.
claims.quote: Conor hasn't fought in five years.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's mobility and movement have been negatively impacted by his previous leg injury.
claims.quote: takes a lot of what made him great out of the equation his mobility... I feel like probably are very likely to not be there anymore.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's ability to perform high-intensity cardiovascular training like running is limited by his increased weight and age.
claims.quote: he can do less of those types of exercises... His bones are now carrying more weight.

claims.claim: Max Holloway has maintained a higher level of activity and competition against top-tier opponents compared to Conor McGregor.
claims.quote: Max Holloway who's been in there fighting some of the best guys um in the division in multiple divisions at this point.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's past success against Max Holloway involved a heavy reliance on wrestling, which is unlikely to be a viable strategy in a five-round fight.
claims.quote: Connor basically just spammed takedowns, controlled them... Do I expect Conor McGregor to come out and spam takedowns in a fight like this though? Probably not.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's betting market price is consistently inflated due to his popularity rather than his actual probability of winning.
claims.quote: the market is just really oversaturated for Connor. He's got people sitting in the bar saying, 'I don't care if he loses. I'm putting 100 bucks on Conor McGregor.'

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has not won a fight in six and a half years.
claims.quote: he's not won in seven years, six and a half.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has not competed in five years.
claims.quote: He's not fought in five years

claims.claim: Max Holloway has only been knocked out once in his entire career.
claims.quote: Max has been knocked out once in his entire career

claims.claim: Conor McGregor struggles to fight while moving backward under pressure.
claims.quote: Conor doesn't deal good with that kind of pressuring having to fight consistently off his back foot.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's knockout power is less effective at higher weight classes compared to 145 pounds.
claims.quote: all of his big knockouts, they're at 145. He hits you at 145, it's a game over.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint Denis has a history of recurring staph infections that negatively impact his cardio.
claims.quote: he's known for having these staff infections

claims.claim: Benoit Saint Denis shows signs of a staph infection on his ankle in recent training footage.
claims.quote: you watch some training footage of him now, some pictures of him now and it looks like he's got a staff infection on his ankle.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis has a recurring issue with staph infections that negatively impacts his cardio in later rounds.
claims.quote: he's known for having these staff infections so he fought Justin Pory he had a staff infection and he gassed out in the second round.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis currently has a staph infection on his ankle.
claims.quote: you watch some training footage of him now, some pictures of him now and it looks like he's got a staff infection on his ankle.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett has weak takedown defense.
claims.quote: Patty Pimble has got a lot of good qualities to him, but his takedown defense is mad weak.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett's striking has significantly improved, specifically his uppercut and overhand right.
claims.quote: Striking has definitely advanced. It's improved a whole lot. He's got a really nice uppercut and he's got a it's like a chopping right hand over the top.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett demonstrated high durability and heart in his five-round fight against Justin Gaethje.
claims.quote: He got dropped a couple times. He'd get back up. He's rocked. Gachi jumps on top of him. He gets back up. Showed his heart. Showed durability.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis tends to wear significant visible damage during his fights, which can negatively influence judges in close rounds.
claims.quote: most of his fights he wears a lot of damage. Whether he's bleeding, whether his eyes starts to swell shut, he's one of those guys that if these rounds are close and he's all busted up, they're going to give it to the other guy.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett lacks high-level offensive wrestling.
claims.quote: Patty doesn't have great offensive wrestling.

claims.claim: Cory Sandhagen has improved his defensive wrestling ability.
claims.quote: Cory Sanhagen's worked a lot on his defensive wrestling.

claims.claim: Cory Sandhagen is difficult to hold down once taken to the ground.
claims.quote: what you'll see is these guys take him down, but they struggle mightily to hold him down.

claims.claim: Mario Bautista is a lesser wrestler than Merab Dvalishvili and Umar Nurmagomedov.
claims.quote: Mario's not gonna chain wrestle him the way Morab Devagei did. Mario's not gonna chain wrestle him the way Umar Nurmagomedov did. He's a lesser wrestler.

claims.claim: Cory Sandhagen is a more polished and refined striker than Mario Bautista.
claims.quote: I feel like Cory Sanhagen is a more polished, refined, better striker.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval has high-level cardio.
claims.quote: He's got cardio for days.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval's durability is declining due to accumulated damage.
claims.quote: I think when you've got 26 professional fights and you train really really hard and you fight really, really hard, the damage starts to compound.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval has been knocked down four times in his UFC career.
claims.quote: He's been knocked down four times in the UFC.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval utilizes an open striking style that results in him absorbing a high volume of strikes.
claims.quote: he's got an open style where he gets hit a lot.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval has high-level cardio.
claims.quote: He's got cardio for days.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval has been knocked down four times in the UFC.
claims.quote: He's been knocked down four times in the UFC.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval's durability is declining due to compounding damage from his career.
claims.quote: I'm not really trusting his durability.

claims.quote: I do rock with his striking and think he's got legitimate power

claims.quote: he's also a guy that in theory could get swarmed in this fight and gas out. He's kind of shown that as well.

claims.claim: Bobby Green has durability issues.
claims.quote: Bobby Green has some durability issues on his own

claims.claim: Terence McKenna possesses a level of fury that allows him to finish opponents in the first three minutes.
claims.quote: he comes with a level of fury that on his best night, he can probably take out anybody in that first three minutes.

claims.claim: Terence McKinney has the ability to finish any opponent in the first three minutes of a fight.
claims.quote: he comes with a level of fury that on his best night, he can probably take out anybody in that first three minutes.

claims.claim: Terence McKinney lacks heart and tends to quit when fights extend past the initial burst.
claims.quote: do you think Terren McKinn's got the heart of a lion? It's like, no, I think he's an athletic specimen with really good potent power and he's a submission threat early on, but like, you know, heart's maybe the one thing he's lacking.

claims.claim: Bobby Green's reflexes and timing are declining due to his age of 39.
claims.quote: at 39 years old, I feel like his reflexes and his timing are going to start to slow down.

claims.claim: Bobby Green has a history of being finished in the first round.
claims.quote: he's already been finished five times in the UFC in the very first round.

claims.claim: Terence McKinney is a 'glass cannon' who cannot take damage well.
claims.quote: he's a total glass cannon. He can dish it out. Can't exactly take it.

claims.claim: Bobby Green has developed a viable submission game late in his career.
claims.quote: no one thinks of him as a submission guy, but one, he just cashed the submission problem in his last fight against Jeremy Stevens.

claims.claim: Robert Whittaker is 38 years old.
claims.quote: He's 38 years old

claims.quote: I think some of these fights uh they've just taken their tolls on them.

claims.quote: he sounds he sounds different. He's a little more punchy.

claims.claim: Nikita Krylov has a questionable chin.
claims.quote: Chin has always been questionable.

claims.quote: I just feel like Kryov kind of lacks the heart, you know... Once the pressure gets turned up on him, he's kind of looking for a way out.

claims.claim: Nikita Krylov is a wild and reckless fighter who leaves openings.
claims.quote: Krylo is wild and reckless. He leaves openings.

claims.quote: that move from 185 to 205, those 20 pounds, it's just like you are in a different human body frame category making that move up.

claims.quote: I struggle to see this lasting more than three minutes to be perfectly honest.

claims.quote: Ellison is a handpicked opponent.

claims.claim: Cody Garbrandt's durability has significantly diminished due to the accumulation of damage over his career.

But then that last fight of his against Josh Walker, he drops Josh Walker. Walker gets up and just puts a little right hand down the middle, zaps his head right back, he hits the deck.
